story.asA: a developer learning the new architecture
story.iWant: detailed documentation and examples for all framework patterns
story.soThat: I can understand and implement dependency injection and service composition effectively
story.tasks: - [ ] Task 1: Create comprehensive developer guide foundation (AC: #1) - [ ] Create `/docs/developer-guide.md` with overview and architecture introduction - [ ] Document core concepts: EndorService vs EndorHybridService patterns - [ ] Explain dependency injection benefits and implementation approach - [ ] Add navigation structure and table of contents for easy reference - [ ] Task 2: Create before/after migration examples (AC: #2) - [ ] Document old tightly-coupled patterns vs new DI patterns - [ ] Show concrete service creation examples: before and after refactor - [ ] Demonstrate service composition: embedding services with DI - [ ] Include performance impact comparisons and benchmarks - [ ] Task 3: Document comprehensive testing strategies (AC: #3) - [ ] Cover unit testing with mock dependencies from `sdk/testutils/` - [ ] Document integration testing patterns with test databases - [ ] Explain service composition testing with hierarchical mocks - [ ] Reference lifecycle testing patterns from Epic 3 completion - [ ] Task 4: Create step-by-step tutorial series (AC: #4) - [ ] "Building Your First Service": Basic EndorService with DI - [ ] "Advanced Composition": Multi-service hierarchies and patterns - [ ] "Testing Strategies": Unit, integration, and composition testing - [ ] "Performance Optimization": Best practices and common pitfalls - [ ] Task 5: Implement automatic API documentation generation (AC: #5) - [ ] Set up documentation generation from Go source code comments - [ ] Generate interface documentation: EndorServiceInterface, etc. - [ ] Create automated doc building in CI/CD pipeline - [ ] Ensure documentation stays current with code changes - [ ] Task 6: Validate and test all code examples (AC: #6) - [ ] Create test suite for all documentation code examples - [ ] Integrate example testing into CI/CD pipeline - [ ] Ensure examples compile and execute successfully - [ ] Add automated validation preventing outdated examples

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: 1. **Complete Developer Guide Coverage**: Complete developer guide covers all framework capabilities with clear explanations and working examples 2. **Before/After DI Examples**: Before/after code examples show dependency injection patterns clearly demonstrating migration benefits 3. **Testing Strategy Documentation**: Documentation covers testing strategies with mocked dependencies, including patterns from Epic 1-3 4. **Step-by-Step Tutorials**: Step-by-step tutorials guide developers through common scenarios like service creation and composition 5. **Auto-Generated API Documentation**: API documentation is automatically generated and always up-to-date with source code 6. **Runnable Code Examples**: Code examples are runnable and tested as part of CI/CD pipeline

artifact.title: Product Requirements Document
artifact.snippet: FR21-25 cover developer experience requirements including documentation patterns, error messages, and API documentation generation.

artifact.title: Refactored Architecture Document
artifact.snippet: Custom lightweight DI container with interface-based registration and type-safe resolution patterns.

artifact.title: Epic Breakdown
artifact.snippet: Complete story breakdown for documentation, error handling, testing framework, and development tools.

artifact.reason: Core service interface that needs comprehensive documentation with examples
Hybrid service interface for automatic CRUD with dependency injection patterns
Pre-built mock implementations that should be documented as testing examples
Mock hybrid service for testing patterns documentation
Test builder patterns that simplify creating test services
Testing helper functions that should be documented
Example unit test patterns using interface mocks
Integration testing patterns with real implementations
Example of constructor injection pattern that should be documented
Service composition method showing DI usage in practice

story-context.constraints: - Documentation must demonstrate all framework capabilities implemented in Epics 1-3: interface extraction, dependency injection, service composition, and lifecycle management - Examples must use established testing patterns from sdk/testutils/ package with MockEndorService and MockEndorHybridService - All code examples must compile and pass tests as part of CI/CD validation - Documentation must follow Go documentation conventions and include comprehensive GoDoc comments - Migration examples should show transition from tightly-coupled to interface-driven dependency injection architecture - Performance characteristics and optimization guidance must be based on established benchmarks from previous epic implementations

interface.name: EndorServiceInterface
interface.signature: type EndorServiceInterface interface { GetResource() string; GetDescription() string; GetMethods() map[string]EndorServiceAction; GetPriority() *int; GetVersion() string; Validate() error }

interface.name: EndorHybridServiceInterface
interface.signature: type EndorHybridServiceInterface interface { GetResource() string; GetResourceDescription() string; WithCategories([]EndorHybridServiceCategory) EndorHybridServiceInterface; ToEndorService(Schema) EndorServiceInterface; EmbedService(string, EndorServiceInterface) error; Validate() error }

interface.name: RepositoryInterface
interface.signature: type RepositoryPattern interface { GetID() *string; SetID(string); GetCategoryType() *string; SetCategoryType(string) }

interface.name: ConfigProviderInterface
interface.signature: type ConfigProviderInterface interface { GetServerPort() string; GetDocumentDBUri() string; IsHybridResourcesEnabled() bool; IsDynamicResourcesEnabled() bool; Reload() error; Validate() error }

interface.name: LoggerInterface
interface.signature: type LoggerInterface interface { Debug(string, ...interface{}); Info(string, ...interface{}); Error(string, error, ...interface{}) }

tests.standards: Unit tests use interface mocks from sdk/testutils/ with testify/mock for behavior verification. Integration tests use real implementations with build tags. Service composition tests validate hierarchical service embedding patterns. All tests follow Go testing conventions with table-driven patterns where appropriate.
tests.locations: sdk/*_unit_test.go for unit tests with build tag 'unit', sdk/*_integration_test.go for integration tests with build tag 'integration', test/ directory for end-to-end testing scenarios

ideas.idea: Test that developer guide examples compile and execute successfully
Validate before/after code examples show measurable improvement in testability
Test documentation covers all testing patterns from sdk/testutils/
Verify tutorial code examples can be copy-pasted and run successfully
Test that API documentation generation pipeline works correctly
Validate all documentation code examples pass CI/CD testing
